metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2343
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Shisa6 interacts with Dlg4/PSD95 in (fig 3, hippocampal neurons, FRET) and decreases AMPAR mobility through its PDZ-binding domain (fig 4, hippocampal neurons, Single-nanoparticle tracking of surface AMPARs).. Experimental description: "Hippocampal neurons derived from 18-day-old rat embryos of either sex were cultured following the Banker protocol"

Single particle tracking assay in figure 4, exerpt from M&M;
"For endogenous GluA2 QD tracking, rat hippocampal neurons were ..."

24/01/2018 Pim
- Over-expressed Shisa6 is of mouse species: "The full-length coding DNA for exon3-containing mouse Shisa6 was obtained from Genscript as a pENTR4 construct ("
- Annotation to mouse was made based on the overexpressed protein in Fig.4
